name: West African Pot Roast
recipeIngredient: 1 cup water mixed with 1/2 cup peanut butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a 4 to 5 quart ovenproof pot over medium heat.
2. Pat the meat dry with paper towels.
3. Add the meat and onion to the hot oil.
4. Brown the meat on all sides, stirring occasionally.
5. Add water mixed with 1/2 cup peanut butter, tomato paste, and seasonings.
6. Cover tightly and bake for 2 1/2 to 3 hours in a preheated oven set to 325 degrees Fahrenheit, until the meat is fork tender.
7. Lift the meat onto a cutting board.
8. Cover loosely with foil and let stand for 10 to 15 minutes.
9. Skim any fat from the sauce surface.
10. If desired, thicken the sauce by stirring in 3 tablespoons flour mixed with 1/4 cup water.
11. Slice the meat across the grain and serve with the sauce.
